description:
## Goal

Close the interp's `Prim::MakeBitstring` gap so the fz-cty.6 acceptance fixture (and any other bitstring-using code) runs through all three execution paths.

## Background

`src/ir_interp.rs` has a catch-all `_ => "prim {:?} not yet supported"` branch around line 489. `MakeBitstring` (Prim discriminant 17) hits it. JIT and AOT both implement the prim by emitting calls to `fz_bs_begin` / `fz_bs_write_field` / `fz_bs_finalize` (defined in `runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs`). The interp can invoke the same `extern "C" fn`s as ordinary Rust calls — every path then funnels into the same `BitWriter` and `Heap::alloc_bitstring`.

## Changes

### src/ir_interp.rs
- Add a `Prim::MakeBitstring(fields)` arm. Mirror the codegen sequence in `src/ir_codegen.rs::Prim::MakeBitstring`:
  1. `fz_bs_begin()`
  2. For each field: compute the FzValue for the field's source (already-resolved Var or Literal) and the field config (BitType, size, signedness, endianness), then `fz_bs_write_field(...)`.
  3. `fz_bs_finalize()` returns the FzValue bits; convert and yield.
- Audit the rest of `_ =>` for any other still-unsupported prims and surface a separate ticket if more than one variant is missing. Do NOT silently expand scope.

### fixtures/shared_heap_send_large_bitstring/README.md
- Add `interp` to the `paths:` frontmatter list so the fixture matrix runs interp too. Drop the "Interpreter omitted — pre-existing gap" paragraph.

### Tests
- Confirm `cargo test --all` covers the fixture across all three paths post-change.
- If `BitstringMatch` or related read prims are missing from interp too, surface the gap in a separate ticket — keep this commit scoped to `MakeBitstring` unless something is hard-blocking.

## Verification

- All paths green on the existing fixture.
- No regression in any other fixture.
- Clippy clean, warnings clean.
title: fz-cty.7: interp MakeBitstring — close three-path gap
